title: 10 best herbal decoctions for postoperative recovery
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for postoperative recovery are a natural and effective way to aid in the healing process after surgery. These decoctions are made by steeping herbs such as turmeric, ginger, and licorice root in hot water to create a soothing liquid that can be consumed to help alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. By using herbal decoctions, patients can experience improved recovery times, reduced risk of complications, and enhanced overall well-being. For instance, turmeric has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain after surgery, while ginger has natural antacid properties that can aid in digestion and alleviate nausea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric decoctions helps with postoperative recovery because they have potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain. The curcumin present in turmeric decoctions also inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, which can contribute to delayed healing. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to protect tissues from oxidative damage, promoting healthy tissue repair. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, herbal turmeric decoctions can aid in a smoother and more efficient postoperative recovery process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Turmeric's potent anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin, help reduce inflammation and pain after surgery, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of complications.
Antioxidant activity: Turmeric's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage tissues and cells during the recovery process, protecting against oxidative stress and promoting overall health.

Wound-healing support: Turmeric has been shown to enhance collagen synthesis, improve wound strength, and accelerate tissue repair, making it an effective natural remedy for supporting postoperative wound healing.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps with postoperative recovery by reducing inflammation and pain due to its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It aids in postoperative recovery by exerting antioxidant effects, which help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by surgical procedures.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): It contributes to postoperative recovery by modulating the immune response, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing through its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory activities.
remedy_parts: Roots: Utilized for its healing and anti-inflammatory properties to aid in wound healing and reduce postoperative discomfort.
Barks: Employed for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties to prevent infection and promote recovery.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 1 teaspoon of dried curcuma longa root for every 8 ounces of water used.
Wash your hands and then combine the weighed root with 8 ounces of cold water in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture over high heat for 5 to 10 minutes stirring occasionally to prevent burning.
Reduce heat to low and let the decoction simmer for an additional 10 to 20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger decoctions helps with postoperative recovery because they have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce pain and discomfort after surgery. The decoctions' warming effect can also help to alleviate nausea and vomiting, common side effects of anesthesia. Additionally, ginger's natural antacid properties can soothe digestive issues, which are often exacerbated by anesthesia or surgery. By reducing inflammation, alleviating symptoms, and promoting digestion, ginger decoctions can play a valuable role in supporting the body during the recovery process, enabling patients to heal more comfortably and efficiently.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort after surgery, promoting a smoother recovery process.

Digestive benefits: Ginger's carminative and anti-emetic properties help alleviate nausea, vomiting, and bloating common after anesthesia, supporting gastrointestinal function and comfort during the postoperative period.

Antioxidant activity: Ginger's antioxidant compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, combat oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by surgery, reducing inflammation and promoting tissue healing.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, specifically 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate postoperative pain and reduce swelling.
Shogaols: Shogaols, particularly 6-shogaol, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with surgical procedures.

Zingerone: Zingerone, a phenolic compound derived from ginger, has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to mitigate postoperative inflammation, pain, and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most used part due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, aiding in pain relief and reducing inflammation.
Stems: Stems are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing oxidative stress and promoting wound healing.
Roots: Roots are used for their antimicrobial properties, which help in preventing infections and promoting a healthy recovery.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried zingiber officinale root powder and 2 cups of boiling water.
Steep the powder in boiling water for 5-10 minutes to allow flavors to infuse.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl to remove solids.
Allow the decoction to cool for 10-15 minutes before storing in the refrigerator.
Use the cooled decoction within 3 days or freeze for later use as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with postoperative recovery because they can alleviate nausea and vomiting, common symptoms experienced by patients after surgery. The menthol in peppermint relaxes the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms that can occur due to anesthesia or surgical stress. Additionally, peppermint's natural analgesic properties can help ease pain and discomfort, promoting a smoother and faster recovery. By soothing digestive issues and relieving pain, peppermint decoctions can play an essential role in supporting patients' overall postoperative well-being.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Peppermint's antispasmodic compounds help relax muscles and reduce spasms, alleviating post-operative pain and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and inflammation, which can occur after surgery, promoting a faster recovery and minimizing tissue damage.
Digestive soothing properties: Peppermint's digestive-soothing compounds calm the stomach, reducing nausea, vomiting, and constipation that are common post-operative complaints.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation, which is beneficial in postoperative recovery by minimizing tissue damage and promoting healing.
Caryophyllene oxide: A sesquiterpene that exhibits anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with postoperative recovery.
Menthol: A monoterpene that has analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation in postoperative patients.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita are commonly used to make decoctions for postoperative recovery due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita are often used to make decoctions for postoperative recovery as they contain menthol and menthone, which have pain-relieving and sedative effects.

Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita are also used to make decoctions for postoperative recovery due to their ability to aid in digestion and reduce nausea, which is beneficial during the recovery period.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1/2 cup of fresh or dried mentha x piperita leaves and 1 quart of boiling water.
Crush the mentha x piperita leaves to release their oils and allow them to steep.
Steep the crushed mentha x piperita leaves in the boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and consume 1/2 cup as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm decoctions helps with postoperative recovery because its mucilaginous properties soothe and protect the gastrointestinal tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds also help to alleviate pain and swelling. Additionally, slippery elm's gentle demulcent action coats and shields the mucous membranes, promoting healing and reducing scarring. As a result, patients can experience improved digestion, reduced nausea, and faster recovery from surgery.
remedy_properties: Mucilaginous properties: Slippery elm's mucilage helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort after surgery, allowing for easier healing.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Slippery elm has been shown to have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling, pain, and stiffness associated with postoperative recovery, promoting a faster and smoother recovery process.

Soothing and calming properties: The gentle, calming nature of slippery elm's mucilage can help alleviate anxiety and stress related to surgery, promoting relaxation and reducing the body's "fight or flight" response.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain after surgery, promoting a smoother postoperative recovery.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, such as ursolic acid, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help mitigate surgical stress, reduce oxidative damage, and improve overall postoperative recovery.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including gallic acid, have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help prevent infections, reduce wound inflammation, and promote faster healing after surgery.
remedy_parts: Barks: Used to make decoctions to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
Leaves: Used to make decoctions to relieve pain and reduce fever.
Roots: Used to make decoctions to promote relaxation and reduce stress after surgery.
remedy_recipe: Gather one pound of fresh ulmus rubra leaves and twigs and clean them thoroughly with cold running water.
Combine the cleaned ulmus rubra with four quarts of cold water in a large pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and let the mixture simmer for 20 to 30 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl to remove the solids.
Store the resulting herbal decoction in a clean glass container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with postoperative recovery because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root decoction inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, leading to a decrease in inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, the decoction's antispasmodic effects help alleviate nausea and vomiting commonly experienced after surgery. By promoting relaxation and reducing pain, licorice decoctions can facilitate a smoother postoperative recovery, allowing patients to return to their normal activities sooner.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Licorice decoctions have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation after surgery, promoting faster recovery.

Gastroprotective property: The decoction's gastroprotective effects can soothe digestive issues common after surgery, such as nausea, vomiting, and stomach upset, by reducing gastric acid production and protecting the mucous membranes of the stomach and intestines.

Adaptogenic property: Licorice root has adaptogenic properties that help the body adapt to stress, anxiety, and fatigue often experienced during postoperative recovery, promoting a sense of calmness and reducing symptoms like anxiety and insomnia.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin helps with postoperative recovery by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, thereby alleviating symptoms of nausea, vomiting, and pain.

Flavonoids (specifically, isoorientin and isovitalin): Flavonoids help with postoperative recovery by exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-ulcerative properties, which may aid in wound healing and reducing the risk of postoperative complications.

Licoricidin (a triterpenoid saponin): Licoricidin helps with postoperative recovery by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help to reduce the risk of surgical site infections and promote overall recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make decoctions for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help reduce pain and discomfort after surgery.
Barks: They are used to make decoctions for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation after surgery.
Leaves: They are used to make decoctions for their mild anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in reducing swelling and promoting recovery after surgery.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 to 20 grams of dried root of glycyrrhiza glabra for decoction.
Crush the dried root of glycyrrhiza glabra into smaller pieces to increase its surface area.
Combine the crushed root with 1 liter of water in a saucepan and bring to boil.
Reduce heat and simmer the mixture for 10 to 20 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the decoction using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow decoctions helps with postoperative recovery because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated tissues and reduce pain. The mucilage in marshmallows forms a protective barrier on the gastrointestinal tract, preventing bleeding and promoting healing. Additionally, marshmallow decoctions have been shown to improve digestion, which is essential for wound healing and reducing nausea commonly experienced after surgery. This natural remedy can help speed up the recovery process by alleviating discomfort and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow root contains mucilages, which form a soothing gel-like substance that helps to protect and calm the mucous membranes in the body, reducing inflammation and discomfort after surgery.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in marshmallow decoctions help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can occur during the recovery process, promoting tissue healing and reducing the risk of complications.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort after surgery, making it easier for patients to recover and return to normal activities.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages in Althaea officinalis decoctions help soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort in the respiratory and gastrointestinal tracts during postoperative recovery.

Gallic acid (phenolic acid): Gallic acid in Althaea officinalis decoctions exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate postoperative pain, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing.
Glutamine (amino acid): Glutamine in Althaea officinalis decoctions may help reduce gut permeability and inflammation, supporting the recovery of the gastrointestinal system after surgery.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in mucilage, making them an effective demulcent for soothing and protecting irritated tissues.
Leaves: They also contain mucilage, which helps to calm inflammation and promote wound healing in postoperative patients.
Barks: The bark of Althaea officinalis has anti-inflammatory properties that aid in reducing pain and swelling in postoperative wounds.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-4 teaspoons of dried althaea officinalis root and rinse it under cold running water for 30 seconds.
Combine the rinsed althaea officinalis root with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass cup.
Steep the althaea officinalis root mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the medicinal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate heat-resistant glass container to discard the solids.
Store the prepared althaea officinalis decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with postoperative recovery because they promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate pain. The anti-inflammatory properties of calendula, a key component of pot marigold, can help to reduce swelling and discomfort after surgery. Additionally, the antioxidants present in pot marigold decoctions may help to protect tissues from oxidative damage and support the body's natural repair processes. Overall, incorporating pot marigold decoctions into postoperative care may aid in a smoother and more efficient recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation after surgery, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of complications.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of pot marigold can help prevent infections at the surgical site, which is especially important in postoperative care to reduce the risk of sepsis and other serious complications.

Wound-healing: Pot marigold's ability to stimulate collagen synthesis and improve tissue regeneration can promote faster wound healing and closure after surgery, reducing the risk of scarring and improving overall recovery outcomes.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Carotenoids (specifically, beta-carotene): This constituent has antioxidant properties, which help protect the skin from oxidative stress and promote tissue repair after surgical procedures.
Sesquiterpenes (specifically, calendulene): These compounds exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help prevent wound infections and promote a healthy environment for tissue repair after surgery.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which aid in wound healing and reducing the risk of infection during postoperative recovery.
Leaves: They contain antioxidants that promote tissue repair and reduce inflammation, supporting a smoother recovery process.
Roots: They possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help in preventing infection and promoting wound healing after surgery.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 dried calendula officinalis flowers and 1 quart of boiling water for decoction.
Combine the dried flowers with the boiling water in a saucepan and let steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl.
Discard the solids and allow the liquid to cool to room temperature for 30 minutes.
Transfer the cooled decoction to a clean glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator for up to 5 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal decoctions helps with postoperative recovery because it contains berberine, a compound that exhibits potent antimicrobial properties. This makes it effective in reducing the risk of infection and promoting wound healing after surgery. Additionally, goldenseal has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort, as well as reduce swelling and scarring. By supporting the body's natural defenses and promoting tissue repair, goldenseal decoctions can aid in a smoother and more rapid postoperative recovery process.
remedy_properties: Berberine content: Goldenseal decoctions contain berberine, an alkaloid with antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help prevent infections and reduce swelling after surgery.

Antioxidant properties: The decoction's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress caused by surgical trauma, promoting tissue repair and reducing inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Goldenseal's isoquinoline alkaloids, including berberine, can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, alleviating postoperative pain, swelling, and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids (specifically Berberine and Berbamine): These alkaloids have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent surgical site infections and promote wound healing, thereby supporting postoperative recovery.

Phenolic Acids (specifically Gallic Acid and Catechin): These phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce postoperative pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress, facilitating a smoother recovery.

Terpenoids (specifically Ursolic Acid): Ursolic acid has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce postoperative inflammation and modulate the immune response, supporting the body's natural healing processes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high concentration of berberine, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that aid in wound healing.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, similar to the roots, are also used for their berberine content, which helps in reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair.

Leaves: The leaves are sometimes used in decoctions, although less commonly than roots and rhyzomes, as they still contain berberine and other compounds that contribute to healing and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried root of hydrastis canadensis for a single serving.
Rinse the dried root with cold water to remove any impurities and debris.
Combine the rinsed root with 8 ounces of cold water in a saucepan.
Bring the water to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the decoction and discard the solid root material before consuming the liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover decoctions helps with postoperative recovery because they contain isoflavones, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The decoctions also help to improve blood flow, which aids in the removal of waste products and promotes tissue repair. Additionally, red clover has natural antispasmodic properties that can help to alleviate pain and discomfort following surgery. Overall, the antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects of red clover decoctions make them a valuable complementary therapy for supporting postoperative recovery.
remedy_properties: Isomucronulatol: Red clover decoctions contain isomucronulatol, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and pain, helping to alleviate discomfort and promote healing after surgery.

Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogens present in red clover decoctions can help regulate estrogen levels in the body, which can aid in reducing hot flashes, night sweats, and other menopausal symptoms that may be exacerbated by surgical stress.

Saponins: Red clover decoctions also contain saponins, which have been found to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, helping to prevent infections and promote wound healing after surgery.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: Isoflavones, particularly genistein and formononetin, found in Trifolium pratense decoctions, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain after surgery, promoting a smoother postoperative recovery.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids present in red clover decoctions, such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid, exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can aid in reducing oxidative stress and tissue damage after surgery, facilitating a faster recovery.

Saponins: Saponins, including trifolin and trifoliin, in Trifolium pratense decoctions have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help alleviate postoperative pain, reduce the risk of infection, and promote wound healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Trifolium pratense roots are used to make decoctions for postoperative recovery due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Leaves: Trifolium pratense leaves are used to make decoctions for postoperative recovery due to their antiseptic and antimicrobial properties that help prevent infection.
Flowers: Trifolium pratense flowers are used to make decoctions for postoperative recovery due to their antioxidant and soothing effects that aid in relaxation and pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Gather trifolium pratense flowers and leaves from a trusted source in quantities of 20-30 grams.
Combine the gathered plant material with 500 milliliters of water in a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil and then reduce heat to a simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before refrigerating or freezing for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with postoperative recovery because they support the body's natural detoxification processes, promoting the elimination of metabolic waste products that can impede healing. The diuretic properties of dandelion root and leaf help to increase urine production, flushing out excess fluids and reducing swelling. Additionally, dandelion's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds may aid in reducing pain and discomfort, while its antimicrobial properties may help prevent infection. Overall, dandelion decoctions can contribute to a smoother and more rapid postoperative recovery.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Dandelion decoctions help promote urine production, which can aid in reducing swelling and fluid accumulation after surgery, thus reducing discomfort and promoting faster recovery.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in dandelion decoctions have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common complications following surgery, thereby supporting the body's natural healing processes.

Stimulatory property on the liver and gallbladder: Dandelion decoctions have been traditionally used to stimulate the liver and gallbladder, which can help improve digestion and absorption of nutrients after surgery, reducing the risk of malnutrition and promoting a speedy recovery.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds help with postoperative recovery by reducing inflammation and improving blood vessel function, which can aid in faster healing and reduce the risk of complications.

Saponins: Saponins in Taraxacum officinale decoctions have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections and promote wound healing after surgery.

Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides in the decoction, particularly inulin, can stimulate the immune system and promote the production of white blood cells, which is essential for fighting off infections and supporting the body's natural healing process after surgery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of inulin, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of flavonoids, which can help to reduce pain and alleviate postoperative discomfort.
Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high concentration of saponins, which can help to reduce swelling and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 handfuls of fresh taraxacum officinale roots and 2-3 handfuls of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves.
Chop the gathered taraxacum officinale roots and leaves into small pieces to enhance extraction efficiency immediately.
Combine the chopped taraxacum officinale roots and leaves with 2 liters of water in a large pot.
Boil the mixture for 10-15 minutes over medium heat to release the bioactive compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage decoctions helps with postoperative recovery because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing pain and discomfort. The natural compounds present in sage, such as thymol and camphor, have been shown to decrease inflammation and promote healing. Additionally, sage decoctions have a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to alleviate nausea and vomiting, common postoperative issues. By consuming sage decoctions, patients can experience faster and more comfortable recovery, with improved overall health outcomes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can aid in the healing process and minimize postoperative complications such as scarring and adhesions.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, can help alleviate pain, swelling, and discomfort after surgery, promoting a smoother recovery process.

Antimicrobial properties: Sage's antimicrobial agents can help prevent postoperative infections, reduce the risk of complications such as sepsis or abscesses, and promote wound healing by preventing bacterial colonization.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, sage roots are believed to reduce pain and swelling after surgery.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, sage barks may help alleviate inflammation and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out one teaspoon of dried salvia officinalis leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Combine the herb with one cup of boiling water and let it steep for five minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for later use within 24 hours.
Take two teaspoons of the cooled decoction three times daily as needed to aid in postoperative recovery.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock decoctions helps with postoperative recovery because they stimulate digestion, relieve constipation, and promote detoxification. The bitter compounds in burdock root stimulate the appetite, reducing nausea and vomiting often experienced after surgery. Additionally, burdock's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain, while its antioxidants support wound healing and tissue repair. By promoting healthy elimination and reducing inflammation, burdock decoctions can help patients recover faster and more comfortably from surgery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Burdock decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as polyphenols and saponins, can help reduce swelling and pain associated with post-operative wounds or surgical sites.

Antimicrobial properties: The antibacterial and antifungal properties of burdock decoction can prevent infections and promote healing by reducing the risk of bacterial or fungal colonization on the surgical site.

Digestive stimulant properties: Burdock decoction's ability to stimulate digestive functions, such as increasing gastric motility and improving gut health, can help alleviate post-operative nausea, vomiting, and constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the burdock plant are commonly used to make medicinal decoctions as they contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that can help promote digestive health and reduce inflammation after surgery.
Rhyzomes: Burdock rhyzomes have been traditionally used to reduce swelling and inflammation, making them an effective ingredient for postoperative recovery by reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried arctium lappa roots in the morning after the dew has evaporated weighing 30-50 grams per serving.
Rinse the harvested roots with cold running water to remove dirt and debris gently pat dry with a clean cloth.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or root cutter to facilitate even extraction.
Combine the chopped roots with 1 liter of boiling water in a heat-resistant container allowing it to steep for 30-40 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and storing in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian decoctions helps with postoperative recovery because it promotes a peaceful and restful sleep, which is crucial for the body to heal and recover after surgery. The sedative properties of valerian root help reduce anxiety and stress levels, allowing patients to relax and calm down. Additionally, valerian's antispasmodic properties can alleviate pain and discomfort, making it an effective natural remedy to enhance postoperative recovery. By promoting relaxation and reducing pain, valerian decoctions contribute to a smoother and faster recovery process.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anxiolytic effects: Valerian's sedative properties can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can be beneficial in managing postoperative pain and discomfort.

Pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian contains compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it effective in reducing pain and swelling after surgery, as well as soothing digestive issues.
Gastroprotective properties: Valerian's ability to protect the stomach lining from irritation can help prevent nausea, vomiting, and stomach ulcers commonly experienced during postoperative recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils and sesquiterpenes, making them effective for reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation during postoperative recovery.
Rhyzomes: Contains a high concentration of valerenic acid, which has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it beneficial for pain management after surgery.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality valeriana officinalis rootstock and dry it properly according to the manufacturer's instructions.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried valeriana officinalis per cup of boiling water and add it to a clean glass container.
Pour 250 milliliters of boiling water over the herb material and let it steep for 10-15 minutes in a warm dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container and discard the solids, reserving the clear liquid.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in an amber glass bottle in the refrigerator for up to 3 days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with postoperative recovery because they possess a unique combination of calming and anti-inflammatory properties. The gentle, soothing effects of lemon balm can help reduce anxiety and stress after surgery, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality. Additionally, the herb's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, supporting the body's natural healing process and accelerating recovery. This natural remedy can be a valuable adjunct to traditional medical care, helping patients feel more comfortable and supported during their postoperative journey.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm decoctions help to relax muscles and alleviate spasms, reducing the risk of post-operative complications such as respiratory distress, pain, and nausea.

Anxiolytic property: The calming effects of lemon balm can reduce anxiety and stress levels in patients undergoing surgery, promoting a sense of relaxation and improving overall mental well-being during recovery.

Antioxidant property: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress, which can exacerbate post-operative complications such as inflammation, tissue damage, and infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots have been traditionally used to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation, making them a great addition to medicinal decoctions for postoperative recovery.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the body after surgery, reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-15 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in clean glass containers.
Boil one liter of water and add the herb mixture, reducing heat to simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container, discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool slightly before drinking 1/2 cup three times daily as needed.
Store any remaining decoction in the refrigerator and consume within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with postoperative recovery because they offer a calming and soothing influence on the body. The gentle warmth of the tea can ease tension and promote relaxation, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress that often accompany surgery. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce swelling and discomfort, while its antimicrobial properties can aid in wound healing. Overall, drinking chamomile decoctions after surgery can be a natural and effective way to support the body's recovery process.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory effects: Chamomile's soothing properties can reduce anxiety and stress, which are common issues after surgery, and its anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate pain and swelling.
Antispasmodic effects: Chamomile's antispasmodic properties can help relax smooth muscles, reducing the risk of postoperative complications such as constipation, nausea, and vomiting.

Hepaprotective effects: Chamomile has been shown to have protective effects on the liver, which may be beneficial in preventing or mitigating liver damage that can occur during surgery, especially when taking certain medications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, chamomile roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling after surgery.
Flowers: Calming and soothing, chamomile flowers ease anxiety and promote relaxation, which is essential for a smooth postoperative recovery.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and store them in an airtight container.
Combine 10 grams of the dried herb with 200 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5 minutes, then stir gently to release any remaining particles.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding the solids.
Store the cooled decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours and consume 1 cup twice daily postoperatively.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai decoctions helps with postoperative recovery because it possesses a unique combination of bioactive compounds that promote healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate pain. Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of dong quai decoctions help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by surgery, reducing the risk of complications and promoting overall recovery.

Vasodilatory and circulatory stimulant: Dong quai's ability to dilate blood vessels and stimulate circulation helps improve blood flow, which can aid in tissue repair, reduce swelling, and promote healing after surgery.

remedy_recipe: Prepare 6-8 grams of dried angelica sinensis roots in a clean glass container.
Measure 2 cups of boiling water and slowly pour it over the prepared herbs.
Allow the mixture to steep for 20-30 minutes, then strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve.
Discard the solids and transfer the decoction to a clean glass bottle, ready for consumption.
Refrigerate the prepared decoction at 2-8°c for up to 24 hours before drinking one cup twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort decoctions helps with postoperative recovery because it possesses analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties that aid in alleviating pain, reducing swelling, and preventing infection. The decoction can be consumed as a tea or added to warm compresses to soothe the body and promote relaxation. Additionally, St John's wort has been shown to stimulate circulation, which helps to reduce postoperative bruising and promotes healing by increasing blood flow to the affected areas.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: St. John's Wort contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce oxidative stress, inflammation, and tissue damage caused by surgery, promoting faster healing and reducing pain.

Pain relief and analgesic properties: The decoction of St. John's Wort has been shown to have analgesic and antinociceptive effects, which can help manage postoperative pain without the risk of addiction or side effects associated with opioids.

Wound-healing and antimicrobial properties: The antibacterial and antifungal compounds present in St. John's Wort decoction can aid in wound healing by reducing the risk of infection and promoting tissue repair, while also helping to prevent scarring.

Barks: The barks of St. John's Wort have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, which can aid in the postoperative recovery process.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh or dried hypericum perforatum herb and clean it thoroughly with a soft brush.
Combine the prepared hypericum perforatum with 4 cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 20 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse into the liquid and release their active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Store the hypericum perforatum decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze it for up to 2 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean 20g of dried eleutherococcus senticosus root material free from debris and contaminants.
Grind the herb into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Combine 5g of the powdered herb with 500ml of boiling water in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes before straining through cheesecloth or a tea infuser.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in an airtight container refrigerated at 2-8°c.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to postoperative recovery that are treated with herbal decoctions are inflammatory conditions such as arthritis, gout, and sciatica. Examples of herbs used in these decoctions include turmeric, ginger, and willow bark, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can provide relief from discomfort and promote overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with postoperative recovery is a blend of Ginger, Turmeric, and Licorice root. Ginger decoction aids in digestion and reduces nausea, while Turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties alleviate pain and swelling. Licorice root soothes the stomach and promotes wound healing.
